reason: MISSING SMOOTHNESS HYPOTHESIS FOR INTEGRATION BY PARTS (lines 397-404): The integration by parts step assumes that p^alpha(tau) is differentiable (or at least absolutely continuous) so that the formula integral(u dv) = [uv] - integral(v du) applies with u=p^alpha and dv=(d/dtau)[phi(gamma(tau))] dtau. For a free particle, dp^alpha/dtau=0, so p^alpha is constant and trivially smooth. However, the general force case (eq conservation-force) requires dp^alpha/dtau to be at least locally integrable. The proof does not state any smoothness or regularity hypothesis on the four-momentum p^alpha(tau) beyond it being defined along the worldline. Since p^alpha = m gamma-dot^alpha and gamma is assumed smooth in Definition 1 (line 46-47), p^alpha is automatically smooth for constant rest mass m. But this chain of reasoning is not stated in the proof. For completeness, the integration by parts step should reference the smoothness of gamma (and hence p^alpha) from the worldline definition.
